CreateOS

CreateOS

Maker

📌

Hey Product Hunt 👋

I'm Pratik, CTO and co-founder at CreateOS.

CreateOS Sandbox gives every AI agent its own isolated environment with its own guest kernel. Egress is enforced in-kernel via eBPF, from outside the sandbox. Code running inside cannot route around the policy, even when fully compromised.

What's in it:

eBPF egress control — allowlist by host, IP, or CIDR, enforced in kernel

Fork — snapshot a running sandbox, clone it in milliseconds

Encrypted p2p mesh networking, plus VPN back to your local machine

BYO-S3 — mount any S3 service as a shared disk across sandboxes

CLI integration — `stripe projects add createos/project` provisions a project with credentials already in your .env [...] Maker

Super proud of the entire team for shipping this 🤩🎉

As the marketing lead here at CreateOS, seeing all the hard work go into building CreateOS Sandbox for secure, high-performance AI agent isolation has been incredible.

Can't wait to see what amazing agents developers build with sub-30ms provisioning and bulletproof eBPF egress control. Let us know what you think.

Report

Naved Alam

CreateOS

CreateOS

Maker

If your AI agents run code you don't fully trust, this is worth a look. Each agent gets its own isolated sandbox with a real guest kernel, ready in about 30ms, so isolation stops being a tradeoff against speed.

results.content:
CreateOS Sandbox

# CreateOS Sandbox

Instant, hardware Isolated Sandboxes for AI agents

## About CreateOS Sandbox [...] CreateOS Sandbox offers AI developers a revolutionary way to rapidly deploy secure, hardware-isolated environments for testing and running AI agents. With an astonishing setup time of approximately 30 milliseconds (p90), it enables near-instantaneous creation of dedicated sandboxes that are both lightweight and highly secure. The platform is designed for AI practitioners, researchers, and developers who need to iterate quickly without compromising on security or performance. Its comprehensive suite includes a powerful CLI, SDKs, over 50 real-world examples, Claude plugins, and integration with ComputeSDK, making it a versatile tool for building robust AI solutions. What sets CreateOS Sandbox apart is its speed and isolation capabilities, allowing for scalable, secure, and efficient AI [...] allowing for scalable, secure, and efficient AI agent development and testing in isolated hardware environments, streamlining workflows and reducing setup times significantly.

# The Best AI Sandbox Alternatives

CreateOS Sandbox is an isolated, benchmarked runtime priced at the E2B and Daytona reference rate, with no egress fees, pause to zero with your state preserved, and a published cost and margin. Here is how it compares, on pricing verified June 2026.

Book a demoSandbox pricing

## Side-by-Side Comparisons

Each comparison uses rates verified June 2026 and links to a full head-to-head breakdown.

### CreateOS Sandbox vs AWS Lambda MicroVMs

AWS's serverless Firecracker micro-VM service for running user and AI-generated code, generally available since June 2026.

CreateOS

$0.0504 /vCPU-hr

AWS Lambda MicroVMs

Varies

Compare### CreateOS Sandbox vs E2B [...] Is there a CreateOS Sandbox alternative to AWS Lambda MicroVMs?

Yes. Both run user and AI-generated code in Firecracker micro-VMs that suspend when idle and resume warm. CreateOS Sandbox adds fork of a diverged paused state into N branches, private sandbox-to-sandbox overlay networks for multi-node clusters, no egress fees, and full self-host of the control plane and storage. AWS counters with general availability today, its existing compliance programs, and larger MicroVMs up to 16 vCPU and 32 GB.

How current are these prices?

All competitor rates were verified in June 2026 and are dated on each comparison. Sandbox pricing changes often, so we re-verify before publishing.

results.title: CubeSandbox: Hardware-Isolated Sandboxes for AI Agents
results.content:
# CubeSandbox: Hardware-Isolated Sandboxes for AI Agents
## Full Stack
2020 subscribers
11 likes

### Description
180 views
Posted: 10 Jul 2026
CubeSandbox by Tencent Cloud is a high-performance, open-source sandbox service that creates hardware-isolated MicroVMs for AI agents in under 60 milliseconds with less than 5MB of memory overhead. It's a drop-in replacement for E2B.

In this tutorial, we cover:

⚡ Sub-60ms cold boot with KVM MicroVMs
🔒 Hardware-level isolation — dedicated kernel per sandbox
🛡️ CubeEgress L7 proxy with credential vault
🌐 CubeVS eBPF network — zero-copy data plane
📸 CubeCoW snapshot/clone/rollback at O(1)
⏱️ AutoPause/AutoResume for cost optimization
🤝 Full E2B SDK compatibility
🔧 One-command install + Terraform deploy [...] overhead barely moves. That means linear density scaling in a hardware isolated world which was previously impossible. Under the hood, Cube Sandbox runs Rust VMM with KVM microVMs. Each sandbox gets its own dedicated Linux kernel. There is no shared kernel and therefore no kernel level escape surface combined with sec hardening on the hypervisor. The isolation boundary is genuinely hardware enforced. Templates are pre- snapshotted OCI images. When you create a sandbox, the system restores a memory snapshot instead of booting an operating system from scratch. That is where the sub60 millisecond boot time comes from. No BIOS, no kernel in it, no systemd, just instant restore. CubeW is the storage engine that powers this instant boot. It uses the XFS reflink ioctal called Floone for copy [...] AI agents. Each sandbox boots in under 60 milliseconds. 60 milliseconds that is faster than a human blink. Each sandbox consumes less than 5 megabytes of RAM overhead. That means you can deploy thousands of sandboxes on a single node. This density is unprecedented in the hardware virtualized sandboxing space. It makes the economics of running agent workloads practical at scale. Cube Sandbox is listed in the CNCF AAI native landscape. It is fully open-source under the Apache 2.0 license built by the team at Tensent Cloud with years of production virtualization experience. The project is actively maintained with releases every few weeks and it is a fully dropin replacement for E2B. You change one environment variable, the API URL and zero lines of your application code need to change. If
